ttl.name: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM (for BOSCH Made)

step1.ptxt: WARNING LIGHT AND INDICATOR LIGHT INITIAL CHECK

atten3.ptxt: Before releasing the parking brake, move the shift lever to the P position for safety (Automatic Transaxle model) or set the chocks to hold the vehicle for safety (Manual Transaxle model).

atten4.ptxt: When the parking brake is applied or the level of the brake fluid is low, the BRAKE warning light comes on.

step2.ptxt: When the ignition switch is turned on (IG), check that the ABS, VSC (without multi-information display), BRAKE warning lights, and master caution, SLIP indicator lights come on for approximately 3 seconds.

atten4.ptxt: If the ECU stores any DTCs, the ABS, BRAKE, VSC warning (without multi-information display and master caution indicator light) lights, and SLIP indicator light come on.
If the indicator remains on or does not come on, proceed to troubleshooting for the light circuits listed below.

entry.ptxt: ABS warning light (Does not come on)
BRAKE warning light (Does not come on)
VSC warning light (Does not come on)
SLIP indicator light (Does not come on)

step1.ptxt: SENSOR SIGNAL CHECK BY TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK) (INTELLIGENT TESTER)

atten4.ptxt: If the ignition switch is turned from on (IG) to on (ACC) or off during Test Mode (signal check), DTCs recorded during the signal check function will be erased.

step3.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Check that the steering wheel is centered and move the shift lever to the P position (Automatic Transaxle) or apply the parking brake (Manual Transaxle).
Set the intelligent tester to Test Mode (select "SIGNAL CHECK").

atten4.ptxt: Refer to the intelligent tester operator's manual for further details.

step3.ptxt: Check that the ABS warning light comes on for several seconds and then blinks in Test Mode.

atten4.ptxt: If the ABS warning light does not blink, inspect the ABS warning light circuit.

entry.ptxt: ABS warning light (Does not come on)

step3.ptxt: Accelerate the vehicle to a speed of 28 mph (45 km/h) or more for several seconds and check that the ABS warning light goes off when the brake pedal is depressed.

item.ptxt: The sensor check may not be completed if wheelspin occurs, or if the steering wheel is turned during this check.
The ABS warning light goes off when the sensor signal check has been completed and the brake pedal is depressed.
The ABS warning light comes on immediately after a malfunction has been detected during the speed sensor signal check.
The speed sensor check may not be completed if the speed sensor check is started while turning the steering wheel or spinning the wheels.
If the signal check has not been completed, the ABS warning light will blink while driving and the ABS system will not operate.

step2.ptxt: Read the DTC(s) by following the tester screen.

item.ptxt: If only the DTCs are displayed, repair the malfunction area and clear the DTCs.
If the DTCs or Test Mode codes (DTC of signal check function) are displayed, repair the malfunction area, clear the DTCs and perform the Test Mode inspection.

atten4.ptxt: See the list of DTCs (See procedure "A").

step1.ptxt: SENSOR SIGNAL CHECK BY TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK) (SST CHECK WIRE)

atten4.ptxt: If the ignition switch is turned from on (IG) to on (ACC) or off during Test Mode (signal check), DTCs recorded during the signal check will be erased.

step3.ptxt: Check that the steering wheel is centered and move the shift lever to the P position (Automatic Transaxle) or apply the parking brake (Manual Transaxle).
Using SST, connect terminals TS and CG of the DLC3.
Check that the ABS warning light comes on for several seconds and then blinks in Test Mode.

atten4.ptxt: If the ABS warning light does not blink, inspect the TS and CG terminal circuit, and ABS warning light circuit.

entry.ptxt: ABS warning light circuit (Does not come on)

step3.ptxt: Accelerate the vehicle to a speed of 28 mph (45 km/h) or more for several seconds and check that the ABS warning light goes off when the brake pedal is depressed.

item.ptxt: The sensor check may not be completed if wheelspin occurs, or if the steering wheel is turned during this check.
The ABS warning light goes off when the sensor signal check has been completed and the brake pedal is depressed.
The ABS warning light comes on immediately after a malfunction has been detected during the speed sensor signal check.
The speed sensor check may not be completed if the speed sensor check is started while turning the steering wheel or spinning the wheels.
If the signal check has not been completed, the ABS warning light will blink while driving and the ABS system will not operate.

step2.ptxt: Using SST, connect terminals TC and CG of the DLC3.
Count the number of blinks of the ABS warning light.

item.ptxt: If only the DTCs are displayed, repair the malfunction area and clear the DTCs.
If the DTCs or Test Mode codes (DTC of signal check function) are displayed, repair the malfunction area, clear the DTCs and perform the Test Mode inspection.
If more than 1 malfunction is detected at the same time, the lowest numbered code will be displayed first.
See the list of DTCs (See procedure "A").

step2.ptxt: After performing the check, disconnect the SST from terminals TS and CG, and TC and CG of the DLC3, and turn the ignition switch off.
Turn the engine switch on (IG) to cancel the Test Mode.

item.ptxt: If the ignition switch is not turned on (IG) after the SST is removed from the DLC3, the previous Test Mode will continue.
If the ignition switch is turned on (IG) with terminals TS and CG shorted, the previous Test Mode will continue.

step1.ptxt: DTC OF TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK) FUNCTION (Procedure "A")

entry.ptxt: Low output signal of front speed sensor RH
Low output signal of front speed sensor LH
Low output signal of rear speed sensor RH
Low output signal of rear speed sensor LH
Abnormal change in output signal of front speed sensor RH
Abnormal change in output signal of front speed sensor LH
Abnormal change in output signal of rear speed sensor RH
Abnormal change in output signal of rear speed sensor LH

atten4.ptxt: The codes in this table are output only in Test Mode (signal check).

item.ptxt: Engine variant learning is automatically performed immediately after the Test Mode is entered.
The ABS, VSC, BRAKE, and SLIP indicators come on for 3 seconds after learning.
